⚠Lap Diagnostics
NO SYMPTOMS
SETTING DIRECTION × DISCIPLINE BASELINES · 23 PARAMS
SettingHigher →Lower → 🔥 Drift 🏁 Road 🌿 Dirt 🚦 Drag 🌃 Street ⛰️ Touge
Alignment
Camber front▲Tops lean out, straight-line stability▼Tops lean in, more cornering grip−3.0° to −5.0°−1.0° to −1.5°−0.5° to −1.0°−0.3° to −1.0°−1.0° to −2.0°−1.5° to −2.5°
Camber rear▲Tops lean out▼More cornering grip−1.0° to −2.0°−0.5° to −1.0°0° to −0.5°0° to +1.0°−0.5° to −1.0°−1.0° to −1.5°
Toe front▲Toe-out, sharper turn-in▼Toe-in, more stability0.0° to +0.2°−0.1° to 0.0°0.0° to +0.2°0.0°−0.1° to 0.0°0.0° to +0.1°
Toe rear▲Toe-out, rear agility▼Toe-in, stability+0.2° to +0.5°0.0° to +0.1°0.0° to +0.1°0.0°0.0° to −0.1°0.0° to −0.1°
Front caster▲Stability, self-centering▼Lighter steering4.0° to 5.5°5.5° to 7.0°4.5° to 6.0°6.5° to 7.0°6.0° to 7.0°6.0° to 7.0°
Antiroll Bars
Front ARB▲Less roll, more understeer▼More roll, front rotatesSoft (5–12)Medium (18–28)Soft (8–16)Stiff (28–40)Medium (16–26)Soft (10–20)
Rear ARB▲Less roll, more oversteer▼More roll, rear plantedStiff (28–40)Medium (22–35)Soft (10–22)Stiff (28–40)Medium (20–32)Soft–med (12–22)
Springs & Ride Height
Springs front▲Less roll — understeer▼More front gripSoft–medMed–stiffSoftSoftMediumSoft–med
Springs rear▲Less roll — oversteer▼More rear gripMed–stiffMed–stiffSoftSoftMediumMedium
Ride height front▲Higher CoG — understeer▼Lower CoG, more front grip8–10 cm9–11 cm13–17 cmMax10–13 cm9–12 cm
Ride height rear▲Higher CoG — oversteer▼Lower CoG, more rear grip9–11 cm10–12 cm14–18 cmMax11–14 cm10–13 cm
Dampers
Rebound▲Extends slowly, stays planted▼Snaps back, can bounce6–98–114–7F soft, R stiff7–106–9
Bump ≈60% reb▲Resists compression▼Absorbs bumps4–65–73–4F stiff, R soft4–64–6
Downforce
Front downforce▲Front grip at speed▼Less drag50–100150–22050–1000–5060–12060–120
Rear downforce▲Rear stability at speed▼Less drag, more slide80–130180–28060–1200–6080–150120–200
Brakes
Brake balance▲More front bias, stable stops▼More rear bias, spin riskFront (55–70%)Front (54–60%)Balanced (50–55%)~55%Front (52–56%)Front (52–58%)
Brake pressure▲Stronger braking▼Gentler stops80–90%95–100%75–85%100%95–100%95–100%
Differential
Front accel diff▲Pull together — understeer▼Independent, looser feel5–15%15–30%20–35%80–90%15–30%15–30%
Rear accel diff▲Exit traction, power oversteer▼More open, more rotation70–90%55–75%40–65%~85%55–75%60–80%
Rear decel diff▲Stable on corner entry▼More rotation on entry10–20%15–25%10–20%Any15–25%15–25%
Center balance (AWD)▲Rear-biased, RWD-like▼Front-biased, stable65–80% rear55–70% rear45–60%50–70% rear60–70% rear60–70% rear
⚙ GEAR TELEMETRY · measured livewaiting…
Find a long straight where you can reach top speed. From low speed in 1st, press ● Record and hold full throttle — rev each gear to redline (no short-shifting) through every gear, until you hit true top speed, then ■ Stop and Apply. Redlining maps your full power curve & gear ratios; the hard launch sizes your tires. Captures only between Record and Stop.

SPEED AT REDLINE PER GEAR (measured)
drive through the gears at full throttle…
SUGGESTED GEARBOX → type into game
enter tire size, then drive to get suggestions
▶ shorten = raise the ratio (slider right) for more acceleration · ◀ lengthen = lower it (slider left) for more top speed · ✓ good. Set the Final Drive first, then drag each gear's slider toward the ratio shown. Arrows compare the optimal spacing to what you measured.
GEAR RATIO CALCULATOR — OPTIMAL SPACING FROM POWERBAND
